I have a form with multiple TextFormFields  with different keyboardTypes. Not sure where and how it all started but I know I wasn't seeing any of these errors before the upgrade. 
I'm just concerned if this will cause build issues.  I'm assuming it will not because they are just warnings...

Hello guys. This issue is causing a lot of problems for me. my users are unable to find search results due to the double character when typing space. When you type space in the textfield it is typed twice and effects the search results!. after that the user is not able to delete character. aand the shown error is 
`SPAN_EXCLUSIVE_EXCLUSIVE spans cannot have a zero length`

Is there any temporary work around? It used to work fine in 1.12 version
@Aljabri-Salman - what you're describing sounds like a regression that's unrelated to this issue. Please create a new issue with a test case that demonstrates the problem and we'll look into it.

@HansMuller  This is the issue. It was closed. and referenced above due to dublicate:
[56514](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/56514)
@Aljabri-Salman I've reopened #56514
I've removed the regression label because, after over 3 years, it really (really) doesn't apply anymore.

Okay, I have solved this issue. 

in my case, by mistake I had... 

`final _scaffoldKey = GlobalKey<ScaffoldState>(); ` **inside build method.**

once I moved out from build to class property, Things were good!

Hope this may help some one.

Thanks a lot, mate. This worked for me.

_searchTextFieldController.clear(); fixed the issue.
In my case, I had a text field to search with a close button on the suffix. So clicking the suffix was creating this issue, because I was trying to clear the text field by    _searchTextFieldController.text='', Now, instead I replaced the code with _searchTextFieldController.clear(); which fixed my issue.

So the issue was quite irritating but after some digging here and there I found out a simple working solution. Just check if you are using any "GlobalKey<......anythingState>" like this in the build method or not if yes then remove it from there and put it somewhere else. Then hot restart the app.
Hope this work for someone.
